defaultDocs.title: SOP: Incident Response Lifecycle
defaultDocs.content:
## SOC Incident Response Lifecycle

### 1. Detection & Identification
All alerts from **Wazuh** and **OpenCTI** must be triaged within 15 minutes.

### 2. Containment
If a high-severity incident is confirmed, analysts must isolate the affected endpoint immediately.

### 3. Documentation
Every action must be recorded in the **Handover Log** and **Audit Hub**.

defaultDocs.title: Policy: Shift Handover Protocol
defaultDocs.content:
## Shift Handover Protocol

- **Requirement**: Mandatory for all Analysts and Shift Leaders.
- **Process**: Outgoing team must review all pending alerts with the incoming team.
- **Verification**: Shift Leader must sign off on the Handover Log.

findingsList: Illegal login attempts detected targeting administrative accounts.

Policy integrity optimization required to reduce noise during routine maintenance.
recommendationsList: Optimize detection thresholds to minimize redundant logs during maintenance windows.
Implement stricter account lockout policies on high-value asset systems.
Improve manual verification workflows for anomalous external access paths.

ingestion.title: Inject Payload
ingestion.subtitle: Inject security logs into the system via standard Wazuh JSON payload.
ingestion.jsonPlaceholder: // Paste standard Wazuh JSON array here.
ingestion.success: Payload injection cycle processed and stored!
ingestion.clearSuccess: Local injection payload cleared without affecting external data.
ingestion.clearNone: No local injection payload to clear.
ingestion.confirmClear: Are you sure you want to clear all local injection payloads? External data will not be deleted.
ingestion.adminOnlyNotice: This page is for Admins only. External production data will not be deleted by this action.
ingestion.protocolDesc: DIRECT LOG INGESTION MANDATES STRICT JSON FORMAT COMPLIANCE. ANY DATA SCHEMA THAT FAILS TO ALIGN WITH SYSTEM STANDARDS WILL BE AUTOMATICALLY REJECTED BY THE NORMALIZATION ENGINE TO MAINTAIN DATABASE INTEGRITY.
ingestion.protocolSchema: MANDATORY FIELDS: rule.id (Rule ID), rule.level (Level), rule.description (Description), agent.name (Host/Agent Name), and timestamp (@timestamp).
ingestion.error: Error: Unsupported input format or invalid JSON.
ingestion.empty: Warning: Payload input area is currently empty.

settings.title: CONTROL CENTER

integrator.guideSubtitle: Comprehensive SOC system hierarchy configuration for Wazuh SIEM, Indexer, and OpenCTI Threat Intelligence.
integrator.step1Desc: The Wazuh Manager acts as the control unit. Ensure the API is enabled to allow the {appName} application to perform agent management and rule checks.
integrator.step1Auth: Auth: Use API credentials (User: wazuh-wui / Custom user)
integrator.step2Desc: The Indexer stores all alert logs. Without this, the dashboard will not display "Security Events" in real-time.
integrator.step2Tip: Security Tip: Ensure port 9200 is open in the firewall (ufw/iptables) so the Dashboard application can query the wazuh-alerts-* index directly.
integrator.step1Desccti: This script runs on the Wazuh Manager side to automatically send indicators (IP/Hash) to OpenCTI and enrich log data with threat context.
integrator.scriptDeployDesc: Use the following commands to prepare the execution files:
integrator.activationDesc: Edit the /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf file and add the integration block:
integrator.err401Desc: Ensure Wazuh API username/password or OpenCTI Token is correct. Also verify the user has administrator permissions.
integrator.errTimeoutDesc: Check ports 55000, 9200, and 8080. Ensure no firewall blocks communication between the Dashboard, Wazuh, and OpenCTI servers.

Alert Register with advanced filters, bulk actions, and incident clustering

workflowStages.label: Stage 1: Monitoring

workflowStages.desc: Add threat intelligence context for deeper analysis.

workflowStages.desc: Determine investigation, escalation, false-positive, or closure decisions.

shiftGuard.valStabilityExit: Environment is stable and handed over for continuity

shiftGuard.valKpiVerified: I have verified shift KPIs and SLA adherence
shiftGuard.valInfraHealthy: I confirm all system integrations are healthy
shiftGuard.valComplianceAware: I acknowledge read-only access and audit logging
shiftGuard.auditorNotice: "Observer-level access granted. Platform interaction is limited to investigative oversight. No production mutations are permitted."

tooltips.date: Timestamp of security record creation by Wazuh Manager.

tooltips.alertId: Unique global security identifier for a specific event record.
tooltips.ruleId: Identification number of the triggered technical detection rule.

tooltips.category: Primary incident classification group (Authentication, Attacks, Audit).
tooltips.hostname: Primary identity of the host system under surveillance.

tooltips.status: Current operational stage for a specific incident ticket.
tooltips.analyst: SOC operational officer performing the case investigation.
tooltips.action: Detailed triage workspace for technical analyst activities.
tooltips.severity: Risk urgency level for prioritizing operational response teams.
tooltips.ruleLevel: Technical rule criticality score. High values indicate critical risk.
tooltips.agent: Surveillance endpoint transmitting data to the management node.
tooltips.falsePositive: Notification that appears suspicious but is confirmed safe (benign).
tooltips.incidentCandidate: Security alert with indicators meeting the incident threshold.
tooltips.mitreTechnique: Mapping of Attack Tactics & Techniques (ATT&CK) for threat profiling.
tooltips.fileIntegrityMonitoring: Monitoring of unauthorized system attributes and file integrity.
tooltips.eventCount: Number of specific activities aggregated into one notification context.
tooltips.decoder: Parser that identifies and interprets raw log data streams.
tooltips.totalAlerts: Cumulative volume indicator of all registered digital security records.
tooltips.openCases: Pending incident queue currently under active investigation.
tooltips.alertsProcessed: Notification volume successfully passing initial validation into the triage system.
tooltips.escalations: Total notification records forwarded to advanced investigators for deep analysis.
tooltips.falsePositives: Notifications that appeared suspicious but were confirmed safe (benign).
tooltips.mtta: MTTA (Initial Response Time): Average speed of analyst first interaction with an alert.
tooltips.mttr: MTTR (Case Resolution Time): Average full lifespan of incident resolution from detection to closure.
tooltips.incidentCandidates: Security alerts with technical indicators meeting high-risk thresholds.
tooltips.containmentActions: Volume of digital mitigation procedures (isolation, block) successfully activated by the team.
tooltips.falsePositiveRate: Percentage ratio of safe alerts against total operational volume.
tooltips.totalWeeklyAlerts: Accumulated active alert volume registered in the last 7-day cycle.
tooltips.monitoredAgents: Total count of endpoint sensors transmitting data to the management node.
tooltips.escalatedCases: Ratio of cases requiring Tier-2 handling against total reports.
tooltips.escalatedCase: Alert forwarded to higher-level analyst for deeper investigation.
